item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of the venom suit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even without the Stan Lee stuff, this really captures the essence of Spider-Man, the whole "Anyone can wear the mask" thing. Really love it.

item.title: Grammatical Error
item.content:encoded:
"Why act like a someone who doesn't exist?"

Did you mean to say: Why act like someone who doesn't exist?

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Ok so here is the wording changes if you were curious

The changes made actually make the clinical tone worse than the original wording. The changes you made introduce a strange phrasing that doesn't seem like native-spoken English.

all the other changes are just typos and grammar:

Most of the changes you've made were unnecessary. The "analyzed"/"analysed" spelling is just a UK/US variant, not a typo.

I've reverted the changes and made adjustments to keep the one necessary grammar fix. Please be more careful when attempting to help edit others' works from now on.

item.title: Re: ...comes great responsibility.
item.content:encoded:
ok thank you

EDIT: Ok so here is the wording changes if you were curious, all the other changes are just typos and grammar:

…and is designed to resemble a popular Marvel Comics character called Spider-Man.

When held or observed by a male human being, the subject would be strongly compelled to equip it.

item.title: Re: ...comes great responsibility.
item.content:encoded: Feel free to so so.

item.title: Re: ...comes great responsibility.
item.content:encoded: I noticed a lot of grammer mistakes and some really awkward wording. Are you ok with me fixing them? Just wanted to see if its ok with you first.

item.title: Re: Too topical
item.content:encoded:
Pressed Bunson explained that for me. That one was the least of my critiques tho.
My main pet peeve with this SCP is how "on the nose" it seems to be.

item.title: Re: Too topical
item.content:encoded:
I think Iara didn't have an issue with the image, but rather the fact that the character of Spiderman himself is copyrighted.

That said, since the SCP isn't literally the character of Spiderman, but rather a Spiderman suit that makes you heroic, I don't think it's an issue. There are plenty of entries on the site which have their basis in copyrighted material (e.g. the Sesame Street Live tape that makes you think every character in a story dies, the toy Enterprise starship that actually flies, the reality-warping Seinfeld clones) but are still CC compliant because they're not the literal, actual characters/objects/locations/whatever.

item.title: Re: Too topical
item.content:encoded: What are you talking about? The image I used is compliant.

item.title: Too topical
item.content:encoded:
Few topical articles survive the test of time. I will be the unpopular one here and say I do not like this. It is also not CC compliant, as it uses an already existing character and is not a parody
Too on the nose for me. I downvoted it.. If it was a parody superhero based on Spiderman, then I would upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe it is a bit unfair to say that the article is for “upvote grabs” because what article isn’t like that? I like it because if anyone was going to make an article commemorating Stan Lee, then this would be the way to do it.
+1

P.S. If you celebrated Thanksgiving, how was it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really mind referential articles, but the language here is extremely clunky throughout. The worst example of it is probably "This desire of SCP-4912 wears off after spending a certain amount of time away from it until the instance of SCP-4912-A has become disinterested in wearing SCP-4912."

-1 until some proofreading and editing is done, and maybe after that too (but for different reasons.)

item.title: Re: well...
item.content:encoded: We have an article about Walt Disney's frozen head. I think it'll do just fine.

item.title: well...
item.content:encoded:
… I get that it is a tribute. And for that purpose it is great.

That said: I kind of don't feel comfortable with the fact, that a marvel character (costume plus backstory plus picture) is used here. He in fact is more than a guy, is an idea. An idea protected under copyright and trademark law. I just… it might be ok within the rules of this site and I truly honor your intent but it doesn't feel right to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel the Foundation would be smart enough to make the connection at the end. Nonetheless, this was pretty good. Excelsior!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks. Him finding it in a trash can is a call back to the Spider-Man No More storyline.

Spoilers
- Hide

SCP-4912-A6 is actually a younger version of Tobey Maguire. Just in case you didn't catch that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That interview log was simply outstanding; SCP-4912-A6 really feels like a regular guy who found a Spider-Man suit, and their "Spider-Man is more than a guy, he's an idea" line was truly touching. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I admit that I probably should've waited first. Although I can't change your mind on it being any "cash grab" for votes. I made this purely because I'm a fan of Stan Lee.

You win some, you lose some, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit too soon I’d say. Kinda echoing Nico with the whole cash grab for votes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this idea, so here are a few comments:

I have edited your grammar in some places to make it read smoother.

SCP-4912 compels them to put it on and wear it.

This is pretty redundant. I would pick one description and get rid of the other.

Most of these preventions in crime will usually end up with the SCP-4912-A instance in a state of injury or death, due to the lack of protection other than SCP-4912 itself.

I suggest changing "preventions" to "attempted preventions" because I doubt the instances actually help stop any crime.

Also, there are some sentences that sound awkward and the clinical tone isn't the best. That being said, I really do like the basis of this scip and I hope it does well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't say that I think the clinical tone and dialogue is entirely up to par. But I am a major nerd with a soft spot for the sentimental, so that's an upvote from me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still don't like the dialogue and the end of the article just feels like an attempt to cash in on Stan Lee's death for upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, the day I made this was also the day that he died. I figured I'd finish this first before anything else as a way to honor him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excelsior.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little biased here, but I'm glad this exists. I'm also glad that an SCP honoring such a great man was written so well. Rest in piece. Thank you, MacWarren, Nicolini, Zyn, and Floppy. To be slightly less sentimental, Hell yes, Spiderman
+1

item.title: ...comes great responsibility.
item.content:encoded:
You know, I guess one person can make a difference.

'Nuff said.

- Stan Lee

This SCP was made in memory of Stanley Martin Lieber.

Big thanks to Nicolini, Zyn, and Floppy for reviewing this.

Image was edited by me.

- Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:New_York_City_Mayor_Mike_Bloomberg_at_Midtown_Comics.jpg
